channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Why the hantavirus cruise outbreak is not the next pandemic, according to WHO
item.dc:creator: Lauren Weber, Lena H. Sun, Victoria Craw
item.description: Experts say because this strain of hantavirus typically requires prolonged or intimate contact, it is not as contagious as covid during the pandemic.

item.title: U.S. strikes two Iranian-flagged vessels as tensions continue amid ceasefire
item.dc:creator: Karen DeYoung, Tara Copp
item.description: The Trump administration, sidestepping a widening battle in the Strait of Hormuz, said it was anticipating a reply from Tehran on its latest terms for ending the war.

item.title: Rubio charms in Rome, but he says Trump’s verbal attacks may continue
item.dc:creator: Adam Taylor
item.description: The secretary of state held warm meetings with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Pope Leo XIV after the president’s repeated criticism of both leaders.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire still intact after burst of violence with Iran
item.dc:creator: Dan Lamothe, Karen DeYoung, Tara Copp
item.description: The president expressed optimism about reaching a deal despite the attacks. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the U.S. is expecting a response from Iran on Friday.

item.title: Aides cite friendly meeting as Rubio aims to mend U.S. ties with Pope Leo
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola, Stefano Pitrelli, Adam Taylor
item.description: Relations between the United States and the Vatican are at a low point over President Donald Trump’s attacks on Leo, who is a leading critic of the war in Iran.

item.title: Authorities scramble to limit hantavirus outbreak, trace contacts around globe
item.dc:creator: Victoria Craw, Lena H. Sun
item.description: U.S. officials in at least five states — Arizona, California, Georgia, Texas and Virginia — are monitoring symptoms of seven returning passengers.

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut suburbs, threatening already shaky ceasefire
item.dc:creator: Mohamad El Chamaa, Lior Soroka, Suzan Haidamous
item.description: The airstrike on the Haret Hreik neighborhood targeted the leader of the Radwan forces, Hezbollah’s elite and battle-hardened fighting unit, Israeli authorities said.

item.title: U.S. intelligence says Iran can outlast Trump’s Hormuz blockade for months
item.dc:creator: Warren Strobel, John Hudson, Ellen Nakashima
item.description: A confidential intelligence community assessment delivered to the White House also finds that Iran retains a substantial missile and drone arsenal.

item.title: As U.S. blockades Iran, its oil still flows ship to ship an ocean away
item.dc:creator: Imogen Piper
item.description: Experts say covert transfers at sea are used to obscure the origins of oil that left Iranian ports and the Gulf of Oman before the blockade was imposed.

item.title: Grain dispute splits Ukraine and Israel even as they fight allied enemies
item.dc:creator: Gerry Shih
item.description: Tense relations soured further after Ukraine accused Israel of buying grain harvested by Russia in occupied territory. Meanwhile, Russia and Iran have grown closer.

item.title: Trump and Xi set to meet under shadow of Iran negotiations
item.dc:creator: Cate Cadell
item.description: President Donald Trump wants a lavish welcome in Beijing for a summit with Xi Jinping next week. But the specter of the war in Iran looms large.

item.title: Trump threatens intense bombing if Iran peace deal is not reached
item.dc:creator: Karen DeYoung, Susannah George
item.description: As talks continue and proposals are exchanged, Washington and Tehran still disagree on what has been agreed.
